app.title: MediaLyze
app.subtitle: Technical analysis for large video libraries, normalized into a filterable local database.

scanBanner.refreshHint: During scans, scan progress updates live. Statistics and table caches refresh after the scan finishes to keep the app responsive.

dashboard.liveUpdates: Live updates are active while scans run. Dashboard metrics refresh automatically.

history.title: Historic data
history.subtitle: Daily trend snapshots across visible libraries
history.empty: Dashboard trends will appear after the next finished scan.

libraries.createSubtitle: Path selection is restricted to directories below MEDIA_ROOT.
libraries.createSubtitleDesktop: Select a local folder, mounted network share, or UNC path to analyze.
libraries.name: Name
libraries.nameRequired: Library name must not be empty.
libraries.typeTooltip:
Movies: no series recognition.
Shows: series and season recognition is enabled during scans.
Mixed: series and season recognition is attempted only where patterns match.
Other: no series recognition.
libraries.duplicateDetectionModeHint: Off disables duplicate detection. Filename is fast and approximate. File hash is exact but significantly more expensive during scans. Both stores and shows both duplicate views.
libraries.scheduledTimeTooltip:
Set the time of day at which the daily scan starts. Use 24-hour format (HH:MM), e.g. 02:00 or 14:30.

Make sure the TZ environment variable is configured correctly in your docker-compose or env file so the scan runs at the intended local time.
libraries.watchUnavailableNetwork: Watch mode is only available for local paths. MediaLyze falls back to scheduled scans for network locations.
libraries.showOnDashboardTooltip: Include this library in dashboard statistics
libraries.hideFromDashboardTooltip: Exclude this library from dashboard statistics
libraries.ignorePatternsSubtitle: Glob patterns are matched against the normalized path relative to each library root before files are indexed or analyzed.
libraries.ignorePatternsHint: Run a new scan after changing patterns.
libraries.ignorePatternsTooltip: Pattern setup, examples, and recommended scenarios are documented in the pattern docs linked above.

patternRecognition.title: Folder & pattern recognition
patternRecognition.subtitle: Configure scan-time series and bonus folder patterns plus ignored paths.
patternRecognition.showSeasonHint: Choose whether Shows and Mixed libraries should recognize series and seasons by fixed folder depth or by regex patterns. Every video inside a recognized season folder is treated as an episode.
patternRecognition.showSeasonTooltipAria: Explain show and season recognition modes
patternRecognition.bonusHint: Bonus folder patterns are matched case-insensitively and are always active during scans.
patternRecognition.docsHint: Pattern examples and matching rules are documented separately.
patternRecognition.rescanTooltipAria: Explain when a new scan is needed after changing patterns

libraries.scanWorkerCountTooltip:
Increase this if one large scan should finish faster.

Higher values can noticeably increase CPU, disk, and memory usage. Adjust it carefully to match your system.
Maximum: {{max}}.
libraries.parallelScanJobsTooltip:
This controls how many libraries MediaLyze may scan at the same time.

Higher values increase overall resource usage. Keep an eye on CPU, disk, and memory pressure.
Maximum: {{max}}.
libraries.comparisonScatterPointLimitTooltip:
Caps how many points MediaLyze returns for comparison scatter plots.

Heatmaps and bar charts stay exact; only scatter plots are sampled for readability and browser performance.

Warning: very high values can noticeably increase browser memory use and make large comparison views sluggish.
Maximum: {{max}}.

telemetryPreview.title: Telemetry preview
telemetryPreview.description: Generate the anonymous payload shape that would be sent for each telemetry mode.
telemetryPreview.notLoaded: Select a payload mode to generate a preview.
telemetryPreview.empty: No telemetry payload preview is available.

historyRetention.title: History retention
historyRetention.scopeNote: File history retention only affects per-file snapshots; dashboard and media library trends use the separate media library history.
historyRetention.reconstructTooltip:
Builds approximate past history from the current media set and inferred add dates based mainly on file mtimes.

This can be inaccurate: mtimes are not guaranteed to match when a file was actually added, and deleted or changed files from the past cannot be reconstructed reliably.

Reconstruction only fills as much history as would still be kept by the current retention days and storage limits configured in this panel.

Missing earlier history is filled in, and older existing snapshots can be enriched with trend metrics that were not stored at the time.
historyRetention.reconstructActiveScanHint: Wait until active scans finish before reconstructing approximate history.
historyRetention.reconstructNoChanges: No missing historical entries were reconstructed.
historyRetention.reconstructSuccess: Reconstructed {{libraryEntries}} library snapshots and {{fileEntries}} initial file-history entries.
historyRetention.reconstructSuccessWithUpdates: Reconstructed {{libraryEntries}} library snapshots, enriched {{updatedLibraryEntries}} existing snapshots, and created {{fileEntries}} initial file-history entries.
historyRetention.progressEntries: {{libraryEntries}} library snapshots created, {{updatedLibraryEntries}} existing snapshots enriched, and {{fileEntries}} file-history entries created so far.
historyRetention.reclaimableNote: {{reclaimable}} can be reclaimed from the database file. Physical compaction may wait until scans are idle.

featureFlags.showMusicQualityScore: Show quality score in music libraries
featureFlags.showAnalyzedFilesCsvExportTooltipAria: Explain analyzed-files CSV export feature flag
featureFlags.showAnalyzedFilesCsvExportTooltip: Shows the CSV export button on the analyzed-files view for the current filtered result set.
featureFlags.showFullWidthAppShellTooltipAria: Explain full-width app shell feature flag
featureFlags.showFullWidthAppShellTooltip: Expands the main .media-app-shell container from its default max-width to the full available page width.
featureFlags.hideQualityScoreMeterTooltipAria: Explain quality score meter visibility feature flag
featureFlags.hideQualityScoreMeterTooltip: Hides the bar-style score meter in the analyzed-files table while keeping the numeric quality score visible.
featureFlags.showMusicQualityScoreTooltipAria: Explain music quality score visibility feature flag
featureFlags.showMusicQualityScoreTooltip: Shows quality score metrics for pure music libraries. Disabled by default because scoring currently targets video-focused quality categories.
featureFlags.unlimitedPanelSizeTooltipAria: Explain unlimited panel size feature flag
featureFlags.unlimitedPanelSizeTooltip: Removes the current 4-row panel-height cap in dashboard and library statistic layouts. Panel width still remains limited by the underlying grid.
featureFlags.inDepthDolbyVisionProfilesTooltipAria: Explain in-depth Dolby Vision profile display
featureFlags.inDepthDolbyVisionProfilesTooltip:
Shows detected Dolby Vision profile, level, compatibility, and Profile 7 layer details where ffprobe exposes them.

When disabled, Dolby Vision variants are grouped as plain Dolby Vision for simpler dashboards, tables, and file details.

quality.visualDensityHint: Enter the video storage for a typical 1080p title in GB per minute. Higher resolutions are scaled upward by pixel count, lower ones downward. Example: 0.02 means about 20 MB per minute for 1080p video-only data.
quality.languageCodeInvalid: Enter a valid two-letter ISO 639-1 language code, for example de or en.

libraries.languageHint: Applies immediately and is stored in the browser.
libraries.themeHint: Applies immediately and is stored in the browser.

libraryStatistics.title: Table View
libraryStatistics.subtitle: Choose which metrics appear in the analyzed-files table and which of those columns expose hover tooltips. Drag rows to change the order.
libraryStatistics.name: Name

panelLayout.noMorePanels: All available panels are already shown.

migration.title: Layout updated
migration.description: Some saved layout settings could not be carried over after the update.
migration.invalidJson: The saved layout data could not be read, so the default layout is shown.
migration.invalidLayout: The saved layout format is no longer supported, so the default layout is shown.
migration.invalidItem: Entry {{position}} was skipped because it is not a valid panel.
migration.unsupportedPanel: Panel "{{panel}}" at position {{position}} is no longer available and was skipped.
migration.duplicateInstance: Panel "{{panel}}" used the duplicate layout ID "{{instanceId}}" and was skipped.
migration.comparisonSelectionAdjusted: Metric comparison "{{instanceId}}" now uses {{appliedSelection}} instead of the saved {{previousSelection}}.

series.title: Series
series.empty: Recognized series will appear after a scan.

export.tooltip: Export the full filtered analyzed-files result set as CSV

textMatch.tooltip: Text fields support comma-separated AND terms. Prefix a term with ! to exclude matches, for example !en, external.

validation.quality_score: Use a score from 1 to 10, for example >=8.
validation.audio_bitrate: Use an audio bitrate like >512kb/s or >=1Mb/s.
validation.bit_depth: Use an integer bit depth like >=16 or =24.

duplicates.title: Duplications
duplicates.emptySearch: No duplicate groups match the current search.
duplicates.suppressFailed: Could not mark the duplicate group as not duplicate.
duplicates.restoreFailed: Could not restore the duplicate group.

history.subtitle: Daily trend snapshots from finished scans

scanLogs.triggerScheduledSummary: Started by the scheduled interval every {{minutes}} minutes.
scanLogs.triggerScheduledDailySummary: Started by the daily schedule at {{time}}.
scanLogs.failedFiles: Files that could not be analyzed
scanLogs.coalescedTriggers: {{count}} additional triggers were merged into this scan.

history.empty: File history appears after a scan captures a changed snapshot for this file.

telemetry.saveFailed: Telemetry setting could not be saved.
telemetry.environmentDisabled: Telemetry is locked off by MEDIALYZE_TELEMETRY_DISABLED.
telemetry.settingsHint: Details are available in Settings under Telemetry.
telemetry.releaseNotesChooseFirst: Choose a telemetry mode before closing release history.

modeTooltips.minimal: Tell the Dev which runtime/system you are using, nothing else.
modeTooltips.enabled: Adds rounded usage counts and app settings to inform development. NO private data.

modeDescriptions.minimal: Sends install/runtime/system details only. No usage statistics or app settings are included.
modeDescriptions.enabled: Adds rounded usage counts, media-kind counts, enabled feature flags, and selected app settings.

panel.title: Telemetry
panel.description: Telemetry is anonymous coarse installation reporting. MediaLyze never sends file paths, media names, library names, search terms, raw ffprobe payloads, or scan diagnostics.

preview.title: Payload preview
preview.description: Generate the anonymous payload shape that would be sent for each telemetry mode.
preview.notLoaded: Select a payload mode to generate a preview.
preview.empty: No telemetry payload preview is available.

stats.title: Public statistics
stats.description: MediaLyze has a public statistics page where you can view the telemetry data contributed by this installation and delete all data yourself by using your randomized installation ID.
stats.installationIdMissing: No installation ID has been created yet.

lastPayload.title: Last transmitted payload
lastPayload.none: No user-visible telemetry payload has been sent yet.
lastPayload.noneAndOff: No telemetry payload is available, and no telemetry will be sent while telemetry is off or disabled.
